response: this belief is wrong and harmful it is based on outdated ideas that do not reflect the true potential of individuals with learning disabilities many people with learning disabilities have shown great success in academic areas with the right support and resources they can achieve at a high level learning disabilities do not limit intelligence or ability they may affect how a person processes information but they do not define their capacity to learn and grow with the right tools and encouragement people with learning disabilities can excel in all areas of study and life it is important to challenge this false belief and recognize the strengths and abilities of everyone regardless of their learning differences
